stories.headline: Bitcoin spot volume cracks below $8B, lowest since October 2023
stories.tldr: Bitcoin's daily spot turnover has slipped under $8 billion, the thinnest tape in 18 months and a setup that historically precedes outsized moves.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Liquidity droughts amplify slippage and stop runs, so a quiet $77K can violently retag $80K or $74K once a single whale prints size.
the_details: CoinDesk's April 29 Daybook flags volume below $8B, the lowest reading since October 2023.
BTC trades around $77,160 into Powell's final FOMC, up $818 on the day but still $17K below year-ago levels.
Coinbase premium flipped negative this week, signaling US desks have stepped back from the bid.

stories.headline: StanChart: DeFi 'bent not broken' after $300M industry backstop steadies Kelp damage
stories.tldr: Standard Chartered says DeFi proved resilient after a $300M Aave-led rescue absorbed the $292M Kelp DAO hit and reanchored deposits.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A working private backstop dampens contagion narratives and pushes back the 'DeFi is dead' takes that flared after the LayerZero bridge drain.
the_details: Aave deposits had cratered ~38% and active loans ~31% in the days after the April 18 Kelp exploit.
Industry backstop hit $300M, including Consensys' 30K ETH pledge and BitMine's 101,901 ETH top-up.
Standard Chartered's Geoff Kendrick frames the episode as a stress test that DeFi materially passed.

stories.headline: April 2026 already crypto's worst hack month since Bybit, with $606M lost in 18 days
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols have shed $606M+ to exploits in the first 18 days of April, the largest monthly bleed since the $1.4B Bybit breach in February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Bridge failures and admin-key compromises now drive the loss curve, raising the bar on insurer pricing and accelerating chain-abstraction risk reviews.
the_details: Drift Protocol bled $285M on April 1 in an attack tied to suspected DPRK-linked social engineering.
Kelp DAO lost $292M on April 18 via a LayerZero bridge exploit that stranded rsETH across 20 chains.
Drift plus Kelp account for roughly 95% of April's losses and ~75% of all 2026 crypto theft to date.

stories.headline: Polygon hard-forks today, retooling fees and laying groundwork for next scaling phase
stories.tldr: Polygon is shipping a network-wide hard fork on April 29 to tune performance and prepare the chain for its next round of scaling upgrades.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With POL still trying to defend altseason relevance, a clean fork keeps validators aligned and gives builders a predictable runway into Q3 throughput work.
the_details: Hard fork goes live April 29, 2026, targeting performance improvements and fee mechanics.
Validators and node operators were instructed to upgrade clients ahead of the activation block.
Upgrade lands as POL underperforms the broad altcoin tape with the CMC altseason index at 39/100.

stories.headline: Stablecoin market sheds $892M as Kelp aftershock pulls USDC down 1% week-on-week
stories.tldr: DeFi unwind from the Kelp breach has carved $892M out of stablecoin float, with USDC slipping ~1% on the week and Tether tightening its grip on share.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Stablecoin float is the cleanest proxy for onchain risk appetite, so a Tether-skewed outflow signals defensive rotation, not capitulation.
the_details: Aggregate stablecoin float dropped $892M in the wake of the April 18 KelpDAO breach.
USDT sits at $189.78B with 59.19% market share, consolidating dominance after exits.
USDC fell ~$794M between April 19-26 to $77.79B, a 1.01% weekly drawdown.
